data.title: Trump’s trade war hurts U.S. auto image as China wins over Europe
data.description:
Donald Trump’s tariff crusade is smashing what’s left of the U. S. auto sector’s reputation in Europe. New numbers show buyers across the continent are turning away from American cars and leaning into Chinese brands. That’s data straight from Escalent’s Chinese Automotive Brands Impact report, which ran from May 21 to July 31 and surveyed 1,692 […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/trump-trade-war-us-auto-china-wins-europe/

data.title: White House considering nominating Josh Sterling for CFTC chairman
data.description: According to market news: The White House is considering nominating Josh Sterling as Chairman of the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC). Josh Sterling, formerly Director of the CFTC's Market Participants Division, is competing against competitors including Brian Quintenz, Director of Crypto Policy at a 16z; CFTC Commissioners Summer Mersinger and Caroline Pham; Perianne Boring, Founder and CEO of Digital Chamber; and Jill Sommers, former CFTC Commissioner.

data.title: Fed Chairman Powell: If policy is not in the right place, we will take action to adjust it
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Powell stated that if policy is not in the right place, action will be taken to adjust it. The Fed is paying attention to financial markets, but our focus is on a dual mandate. Before the next meeting, we will monitor the labor market, growth data, and inflation data to determine whether policy is in place. (Jinshi)

data.title: Fed spokesperson: Powell may open up space for further rate cuts
data.description: Nick Timiraos, the "Federal Reserve's Voice," recently wrote: Federal Reserve Chairman Powell said that even after last week's rate cut, he still believes the Fed's interest rate stance "remains slightly tight," suggesting that there is room for more rate cuts this year if officials continue to judge that recent labor market weakness outweighs inflation setbacks. Powell largely reiterated the views he expressed at the press conference following last week's rate cut. He emphasized the challenges the Fed faces in achieving its twin goals of maintaining low and stable inflation and promoting a healthy labor market. "Two-way risks mean there is no risk-free path," Powell said. "Cutting rates too much, too quickly could keep inflation close to 3% rather than the Fed's 2% target, while maintaining a restrictive policy stance for too long could unnecessarily weaken the labor market." Powell also reiterated his view that slowing job growth this summer necessitated last week's policy shift to focus more on the labor market than earlier this year. The slightly tighter rate setting puts the Fed in a good position to respond to potential economic developments. (Jinshi)

data.title: Fed Chair Powell: Rate cut is another step toward a more neutral policy stance
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Powell said that the policy stance remains moderately tight and is well-positioned to respond to potential developments. Inflation remains at a high level, and the interest rate cut is another step towards a more neutral policy stance. (Jinshi)

data.title: Fed Chairman Powell: Fed is in a good position after September rate cut
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell said the Fed is "in a good position" after the September rate cut, and the overall impact of major federal policy changes "remains to be seen." (Jinshi)

data.title: XRP Price Eyes $4 Breakout as Analysts Predict Major Trend Shift
data.description:
The price of XRP is targeting a significant breakout towards the $4 mark, as a growing number of analysts forecast a major shift in market trends that could influence its trajectory. This anticipated movement has generated excitement among investors and traders alike, as expert opinions suggest that this level could serve as a critical pivot point for the cryptocurrency's performance in the near future. As market sentiment evolves and additional insights emerge, many are paying close attention to XRP's potential to achieve this milestone and the implications it may have for the broader digital asset landscape.
source: https://blocknews.com/xrp-price-eyes-4-breakout-as-analysts-predict-major-trend-shift/

data.title: Trump-allied Oracle taps bond market for $15 billion in investment-grade debt
data.description:
Oracle is borrowing $15 billion from the U. S. investment-grade bond market this week, according to Bloomberg. The company is slicing up the deal into seven tranches, including a 40-year bond, which almost no one does anymore. That long-term slice is being priced at about 1. 65 percentage points above similar U. S. Treasuries. The deal is being […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/oracle-taps-bond-market-for-15-billion/

data.title: US Senate Republicans originally planned to review the revised agenda of the market structure bill on September 30, but the deadline may be postponed.
data.description: Crypto journalist Eleanor Terrett posted on the X platform that multiple sources on Capitol Hill agree that the September 30th deadline originally targeted by Republicans on the Senate Banking Committee for revising the Market Structure Act is now effectively impossible. Democrats have yet to provide feedback on the bill, stating they want to agree on a process before beginning negotiations on the details. The impending US government shutdown is reportedly making a meeting on the 30th virtually impossible. A possible new target date is the week of October 20th, but this has not yet been determined.
